topic: calibration-aware active learning for image classification

scope_classification.label: local_experiment
scope_classification.rationale: The topic can be explored with small simulations, synthetic data, or lightweight local experiments on the Mac.
scope_classification.feasibility_note: The research topic is highly feasible on your MacBook M4 with 36GB RAM and Apple MPS GPU, as calibration-aware active learning for image classification is well-suited for this hardware configuration. I recommend using CIFAR-10/CIFAR-100 for initial experiments, or the Places365 dataset for more complex scene classification tasks. Expect initial model training to take 1-3 hours, with each active learning iteration requiring 10-30 minutes, resulting in a total experiment time of approximately 4-12

parsed_papers.title: Calibration of uncertainty in the active learning of machine learning force fields
parsed_papers.core_claim: Calibrating the uncertainty quantification within MEPE will improve active learning performance.
limitations: The predictive uncertainty of a Gaussian process (GP) is unlikely to be accurate or precise immediately after training.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The study focuses on calibrating uncertainty for a Gaussian Process, but it's unclear if these methods would be effective for other types of machine learning models, such as neural networks.

parsed_papers.title: Advance warning of $\gamma$-ray blazar flares from \textit{Fermi}-LAT light curves: a strictly causal machine-learning backtest
parsed_papers.core_claim: The study uses machine learning models (WATCH and TRIGGER) trained on Fermi-LAT light curves with 42 variability features from 365-day trailing windows to predict gamma-ray blazar flares within 90 days (WATCH) and 45 days (TRIGGER).
method_or_focus: A strictly causal machine learning backtest approach that avoids temporal leakage by using only pre-cutoff data before MJD 60000 for all scaling, calibration, threshold selection, and validation.
Fermi-LAT light curves for 13 bright blazars
datasets_used: Fermi-LAT light curves for 13 bright blazars
limitations: No reliable pre-onset TRIGGER alert is obtained
parsed_papers.possible_gap: Based on full paper reading: A strictly causal machine learning backtest approach that avoids temporal leakage by using only pre-cutoff data before MJD 60000 for all scaling, calibration, threshold selection, and validation.

deep_read.method_summary: The study uses machine learning models (WATCH and TRIGGER) trained on Fermi-LAT light curves with 42 variability features from 365-day trailing windows to predict gamma-ray blazar flares within 90 days (WATCH) and 45 days (TRIGGER).
deep_read.key_innovation: A strictly causal machine learning backtest approach that avoids temporal leakage by using only pre-cutoff data before MJD 60000 for all scaling, calibration, threshold selection, and validation.
datasets_used: Fermi-LAT light curves for 13 bright blazars
limitations_stated: No reliable pre-onset TRIGGER alert is obtained
deep_read.title: Advance warning of $\gamma$-ray blazar flares from \textit{Fermi}-LAT light curves: a strictly causal machine-learning backtest

parsed_papers.title: The Design and Impact of Interactive Online Modules for Dental Faculty Calibration
parsed_papers.core_claim: A self-paced, interactive online approach is an effective solution for dental faculty calibration, overcoming scheduling challenges.
limitations: The abstract is incomplete, cutting off during the description of the data collection method (survey), which limits understanding of the study's full scope and findings.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The study relies on self-reported survey data; a gap exists in measuring the actual change in faculty teaching behavior or student outcomes through objective observation.

parsed_papers.title: Mapping water quality in complex estuarine and coastal waters using deep learning models and high-resolution satellite imagery
parsed_papers.core_claim: Deep learning can be used to map water quality in complex estuarine and coastal waters by leveraging remote sensing imagery.
limitations: severe lack of overlapping matchups for calibration and validation
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The effectiveness of deep learning models for water quality mapping is often limited by the scarcity of sufficient in-situ data for training and validation.

parsed_papers.title: Transferability of machine-learning-based global calibration models for NO2 and NO low-cost sensors
parsed_papers.core_claim: The paper claims that machine-learning-based calibration models for NO2 can be effectively transferred across different locations.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The abstract introduces the concept of transferable calibration models but does not provide evidence of their performance when applied in new locations.
